title: Aspidogyne peruviana

The plant is classified as a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows in the ground rather than on trees or other plants. Within the orchid family, Aspidogyne peruviana is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. The classification of Aspidogyne peruviana is based on its morphology and genetic characteristics.

intro: Aspidogyne peruviana, also known as Peruvian Aspidogyne, is a rare and endangered orchid species native to the Andean region of Peru. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aspidogyne peruviana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Peruvian Aspidogyne is typically prepared as a decoction or tincture, and it is also used in modern herbal medicine as a component of various supplements and teas.
